items.title: Anwar on one-day visit to Sabah today
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to make a one-day visit to Sabah today to attend several official and community programmes. The prime minister will begin his visit by attending the groundbreaking ceremony for the Sabah Southern Madani Link project at Dewan Lundayeh, Sipitang, at 11am. After the programme in Sipitang, Anwar is scheduled to return to Kota Kinabalu to attend the Melangkah Bersama Madani: Wanita Beriman, Wanita Bertahan programme at the Sabah International Convention Centre (SICC) at 2.30pm.

items.title: Preserve Lot 642 as a public green space
items.summary: LETTER: The discussion surrounding the proposed development on Lot 642 in Gelugor is about far more than a single piece of land. It raises a broader question that will shape the future of Penang for generations to come. The residents of Gelugor are not opposed to development.

items.title: Onn Hafiz defends Johor BN’s no-DAP stance, says politics should not be conflated with race
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Johor caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has defended his insistence that Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) would not cooperate with DAP if returned to power, arguing that political disagreements with the party should not be conflated with race. Speaking in an exclusive interview with New Straits Times (NST), Onn Hafiz said DAP was a political party rather than a racial group, rejecting accusations that his recent remarks had alienated the Chinese community ahead of the upcoming Johor state election. DAP is a political party that is perhaps seen as championing matters relating to the Chinese community.

items.title: Fadhlina: All technical secondary schools placed under fully residential schools division
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — All nine Technical Secondary Schools (SMT) nationwide have officially been placed under the management of the Fully Residential Schools Management Division (BPSBP) of the Education Ministry (MoE). Under the new restructuring, the schools will now be known as Technical Fully Residential Schools (SBP-T), with a focus on high-performing academic and technical excellence. The technical secondary schools were previously managed by the Technical and Vocational Education Division (BPLTV).

items.title: Sarawak Fire Department recommends shutdown of Lawas highway construction site over methane gas risk
items.summary: LAWAS, June 13 — The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department has recommended that the Sarawak North Coastal Highway (NCH) construction site in Lawas be shut down after highly-flammable methane gas was detected at high risk levels yesterday. The department’s operations centre said that the Lower Explosive Limit (LEL) readings for three days by portable gas detectors near the gas-emitting hole had risen to 100 per cent, while readings within a five metre radius in the area recorded LEL readings of zero per cent. “Advice and confirmation by the Minerals and Geosciences Department, the Department of Environment and the Chemistry Department were also obtained,” the centre said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: PN has yet to receive Reset application to join coalition, says Samsuri
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Perikatan Nasional (PN) has not received any application from the Reset movement to join the coalition ahead of the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections. PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar said there have been no official discussions or efforts to bring Reset, led by former Bersatu deputy president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainuddin, into PN. "There have been no official discussions for Reset to join PN, only informal talks.

items.title: Police probe chair-pulling incident that left Perlis student out of school for nearly four months
items.summary: KANGAR, June 13 — Police are conducting a thorough investigation into an incident involving a 12-year-old girl who fell after a female classmate allegedly pulled her chair at a national school in the Padang Besar area on Feb 11. Padang Besar police chief ACP Mohd Shokri Abdullah said the incident occurred at about 12.15 pm when the pupil was believed to have been attempting to sit down after greeting a teacher who had entered the classroom. He said the police are still awaiting further instructions from the Deputy Public Prosecutor’s Office.

items.title: Police hunt three masked suspects after RM90,000 luxury handbag heist in Cyberjaya
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 13 — Police are tracking down three suspects believed to be involved in a break-in at a luxury handbag outlet in a shopping complex in Cyberjaya, resulting in the loss of 25 handbags worth RM90,000. Sepang District Police chief ACP Norhizam Bahaman said police received a report on the incident at about 12.30 pm yesterday from a woman who owns the premises. “Checks on closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage revealed that three masked suspects dressed in black entered the premises before ransacking the outlet and taking handbags displayed on the store’s shelves,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: SG4 states expected to hold state elections concurrently with GE16
items.summary: ALOR STAR: The four Perikatan Nasional-led states, known as State Government 4 (SG4), are expected to hold their state elections concurrently with the 16th General Election (GE16). Pas central election director Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor said the move would be more effective, streamline party machinery movement and save costs. "My hope is for the state elections in the four states and the parliamentary election to be held together.

items.title: 100 days of the global energy crisis: Why discipline and unity are our strongest shield
items.summary: JUNE 13 — One hundred days into the global energy crisis, one truth is now unavoidable: this is no longer a temporary disruption to be waited out. Malaysia must therefore make a deliberate shift — from emergency response to long-term resilience. Brent crude has averaged roughly US$95–100 a barrel since the crisis began, spiking above US$120 at its peak before settling in the mid-90s — high enough to keep pressure on prices, supply chains and household budgets.

items.title: Helen Mirren, England women's Euro stars among latest UK honours recipients
items.summary: LONDON: Oscar-winning actress Helen Mirren, Black Sabbath guitarist Tony Iommi and six of England's European Championship-winning women footballers were recipients of British honours announced Friday. From the world of sports, rugby league great and research fundraiser Kevin Sinfield was knighted, while former Manchester United footballer Lou Macari landed an honour for his decade-long commitment to a homeless charity. Last year's saw David Beckham receive his knighthood, becoming Sir David.

items.title: Anwar urges Negeri Sembilan voters to return Pakatan to power, backs Aminuddin for MB
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 13 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim yesterday called on voters in Negeri Sembilan to give the coalition a fresh mandate in the 16th state election to continue governing the state. The prime minister said such support was crucial to enable Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun to continue leading the state administration and ensure a more secure future for Negeri Sembilan. “Insya-Allah, Negeri Sembilan’s future will be more secure.

items.title: Don’t betray Pakatan if you are not picked, Aminuddin tells party leaders and election machinery
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 13 — Negeri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has reminded PH leaders and election machinery not to betray the coalition if their names are not selected as candidates in the 16th State Election. The Election Commission (EC) has set Aug 1 as polling day for the Negeri Sembilan state election. The 2023 Negeri Sembilan state election saw PH and Barisan Nasional (BN) form the state government after winning 31 of the 36 seats.

items.title: Sanusi confident of 36-0, 15-0 clean sweep in Kedah
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor is confident that Pas and its coalition partners can win all 36 state seats in the next Kedah election. "Pas will lead a 36-0 victory for the Kedah state seats. "We will seize the Sungai Petani parliamentary seat, making all 15 parliamentary seats ours, and all 36 state seats as well.

items.title: Health Ministry eyes major organ transplant push in 2026 as thousands await lifesaving donors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — The Health Ministry (MOH) has set the goal of advancing the national transplant agenda in a more progressive manner as a national mission for 2026. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad said that although hundreds of thousands of Malaysians have registered as organ donor pledgers through the MySejahtera application, thousands of chronic patients are still waiting for a miracle while on transplant waiting lists. Meanwhile, he also expressed gratitude for the successful liver transplant of a child, Muhammad Raif Rabbani, at Hospital Tunku Azizah after battling biliary atresia since infancy.

items.title: DOE steps up air and water monitoring after hazardous waste fire in Gebeng (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Malaysian environmental authorities have intensified air and water quality monitoring after a fire at a scheduled waste facility in Gebeng, Pahang, amid concerns that pollutants from the site could spread into nearby waterways. The Department of Environment (DOE) said it was alerted to the incident at the Gebeng Industrial Area at about 7.20am on June 12 before dispatching enforcement officers to assess the potential environmental impact. The DOE said it had also deployed air quality monitoring equipment at the nearby Gebeng Industrial Training Institute (ILP) to detect the presence of hazardous gases following the fire.

items.title: Police arrest 13 men over armed brawl linked to debt dispute in Jinjang Utara
items.summary: Sentul District Police chief ACP Basri Sagoni said all the suspects, aged between 18 and 51, were detained within 12 hours of the incident through several raids conducted around the capital. He said the Sentul District Police Headquarters (IPD) District Control Centre (DCC) had received a MERS 999 emergency call from a member of the public at 3.27 pm reporting the incident. Basri said the arrests were carried out by the Sentul IPD Criminal Investigation Division (CID)with the cooperation of the Kuala Lumpur Police Contingent Headquarters (IPK) CID.

items.title: Respond to racial and religious hatred with wisdom, not anger, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — The best approach to deal with hateful and racial sentiments is not through anger, but rather through patience, wisdom and good morals, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said that although Malaysia is often cited as an example of harmony among a multicultural society, the country is not immune to challenges such as racial sentiments, hatred based on religion and race, as well as various accusations that try to divide society. Citing previous incidents of Quran burning, the Prime Minister said Malaysia opted to respond positively rather than react emotionally.

items.title: Loke backs Aminuddin to stay on as Negeri Sembilan MB, says DAP will defend only incumbent seats
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Negeri Sembilan DAP has backed Negeri Sembilan PKR chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun to remain menteri besar should Pakatan Harapan (PH) retain power in the next state election. Aminuddin has served as menteri besar since 2018. “On August 1, we want Tok Min (Aminuddin) to continue leading Negeri Sembilan as menteri besar,” Loke said at the launch of PH Negeri Sembilan’s election machinery yesterday, as reported by the national daily.

items.title: Newly minted trillionaire Musk under fire over Belfast riots
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Elon Musk, newly crowned the world's first trillionaire, faced renewed criticism Friday over anti-immigrant riots in Belfast after researchers said violent narratives he amplified on his platform X amassed millions of views. Musk amplified calls for protest across Britain from anti-immigration activist Tommy Robinson and wrote to his 240 million followers on X: "Only by protesting REPEATEDLY and LOUDLY will there be any change." "Musk's amplification has been instrumental," contributing 64 million views, CCDH said in a report.

items.title: Anwar urges Malaysians to reject racial and religious hatred with wisdom
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said hateful racial and religious sentiments should be countered with patience, wisdom and good morals, not anger. He said that while Malaysia is often cited as a model of multicultural harmony, the country still faces challenges such as racial rhetoric, religious hatred and divisive accusations. Anwar said he met religious leaders and scholars on the sidelines of the 3rd International Summit of Religious Leaders yesterday.

items.title: UDA hands over all 153 Areca Terrace Phase 4B units
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: UDA Holdings Bhd has handed over all 153 residential units under its Areca Terrace Phase 4B development ahead of schedule, with the project achieving a full take-up rate. UDA Land (South) Sdn Bhd acting chief operating officer Norazim Shukor said the positive response reflected buyers' confidence in UDA's products and brand, as well as the company's ability to deliver quality developments. Norazim said UDA had also collaborated with Ikea Tebrau Johor to create a minimalist and functional show unit, helping prospective buyers visualise the property's potential and make informed purchasing decisions.

items.title: Musk becomes world's first trillionaire as SpaceX shares soar
items.summary: NEW YORK: Shares in Elon Musk's SpaceX closed almost 20 per cent higher on their trading debut Friday after the biggest IPO in history, making the polarising entrepreneur the world's first trillionaire as he vowed to take humanity to Mars. The stock climbed as high as US$176, or 31 per cent above its offering price of US$135, in its first session before ending the day at US$161.50. The company priced more than 555 million shares at US$135 each in a Thursday filing with the US markets regulator, valuing SpaceX at just under US$1.8 trillion.

items.title: Trump deportation flight drops Iranians in violence-hit Central African Republic
items.summary: BANGUI, Central African Republic: A US deportation flight landed in the Central African Republic on Friday, lawyers and activists said, carrying nationals from Iran, Afghanistan, Turkey and Georgia as part of the latest "third-country" deportation under US President Donald Trump. The US State Department advisory for the impoverished, violence-wracked CAR reads: "Do not travel to Central African Republic for any reason." She said those headed to the Central African Republic "are mainly withholding grantees from a variety of countries, including Iran, Afghanistan, Turkiye, Georgia."

items.title: Marginal seats could decide Negri polls outcome, say analysts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Several constituencies in Negri Sembilan won by razor-thin margins in the 2023 state election are expected to be among key battlegrounds in the upcoming polls, said analysts. Universiti Utara Malaysia political analyst Professor Dr Mohd Azizuddin Mohd Sani said at least 10 Barisan Nasional-held seats were won with majorities of fewer than 1,000 votes. They include Juasseh, which BN retained with a mere 78-vote majority, Kota (135-vote majority), Sungai Lui (535-vote majority) and Palong (564-vote majority).

items.title: Johor's swing seats under the spotlight
items.summary: Seats won by margins of fewer than 2,000 votes in the 2022 state election — namely Tangkak, Serom, Bukit Naning, Parit Yaani and Kemelah — are shaping up as key battlegrounds in the coming polls. Others, including Kempas, Tiram, Permas, Kota Iskandar and Bentayan, will also become major battlegrounds. Serom witnessed a three-cornered race between BN, PH and PN, with BN's Khairin Nisa Ismail emerging victorious with a slim 699-vote majority.

items.title: Zuckerberg says Meta made 'mistakes' in AI workforce shift
items.summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg has told employees that the social media giant has made mistakes in its AI transformation of its workforce, according to an internal memo seen by Reuters. Zuckerberg is pumping hundreds of billions of dollars into AI as he seeks to reshape his company's inner workings around the technology, reflecting a broader pattern among major US companies this year, particularly in the tech sector. He said Meta will try to find new roles for employees reassigned to train AI models, after the Facebook owner carried out a massive restructuring in May, laying off 10 per cent of its workforce globally and transferring 7,000 employees to new initiatives related to AI workflows.

items.title: Hizbollah claims Israeli troops forced to retreat in south Lebanon clashes
items.summary: BEIRUT: Hizbollah said on Friday its fighters had confronted Israeli forces advancing towards a southern Lebanese town, as Israel pressed on with its strikes in Lebanon. The group also claimed other attacks on Israeli troops in southern Lebanon. The Israeli military meanwhile issued an evacuation warning for three southern Lebanese villages.

items.title: 'DAP is not a race': Onn Hafiz defends Johor BN's anti-DAP stance [WATCH]
items.summary: Responding to criticism over his recent declaration that Johor BN would not work with DAP, he said the party should not be conflated with the Chinese community, adding that his administration's policies were always guided by the interests of all Johoreans regardless of race or political affiliation. "DAP is not a race. DAP is a political party that is perhaps seen as championing matters relating to the Chinese community.

items.title: ‘Teh tarik’ and football: Where Malaysians can catch the World Cup with fellow fans in KL and Selangor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — With the 2026 Fifa World Cup finally underway, Malaysians looking to watch matches alongside fellow football fans have plenty of options across the Klang Valley, from sports bars and rooftop venues to the country’s beloved 24-hour mamak restaurants. Fortunately, football fans will still be able to catch every game live through RTM, RTMKlik and Unifi TV. Closer to the city centre, football fans have traditionally gravitated towards the 24-hour mamak restaurants around KLCC and Jalan Ampang whenever major tournaments come around.

items.title: North Korea condemns US missile sale approval to South Korea, KCNA says
items.summary: SEOUL: North Korea's foreign ministry condemned a US decision to approve the sale of advanced air-to-air missiles and related equipment to South Korea, warning the move would worsen tensions on the Korean peninsula, state media KCNA said on Saturday. The official cited the US State Department's approval of a nearly $300 million foreign military sale of advanced air-to-air missiles and related equipment to South Korea as the latest example. North Korea routinely criticises US-South Korea military cooperation as preparation for war.

items.title: Iran says draft US deal to be signed 'remotely', possibly in 'coming days'
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on Friday that upon finalisation, a draft deal with the United States would be signed "remotely", which could happen "in the coming days." After that, it will be announced that this memorandum of understanding has been signed by both parties," said Araghchi in an interview with state television. On Thursday, US President Donald Trump said he had called off planned strikes on Iran and claimed a deal to end the war could be signed soon.

items.title: Blake Lively can recover legal fees from Justin Baldoni, US judge rules
items.summary: US District Judge Lewis Liman in Manhattan ruled after Lively settled her lawsuit accusing Baldoni of sexual harassment and retaliation in connection with their 2024 movie "It Ends With Us," where both starred and Baldoni directed. Before Lively settled her lawsuit last month, Liman had dismissed her harassment claims and all claims against Baldoni but said she could sue Baldoni's production company Wayfarer Studios for retaliation. He repeated Baldoni's denials of Lively's sexual harassment and retaliation claims.

items.title: Since March, Trump has repeatedly said the Iran war will end soon
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said the United States has reached a deal to end the war with Iran, but the agreement has not been finalised and he has disputed accounts that it favours Teheran. Here are some examples: "We have points of, major points of agreement, I would say, almost all points of agreement. - May 5, Truth Social post "It might not happen, but it could happen any day."

items.title: Contemporary art giant David Hockney dies aged 88
items.summary: LONDON: British artist David Hockney, one of the most influential and defining figures in contemporary art whose paintings captured the world in brilliant colour, has died aged 88, his publicist announced on Friday. [[nid:1461943]] Lauding him as "one of the most important figures in contemporary art in both the 20th and 21st centuries", his publicist Erica Bolton said he died "peacefully at home" in London on Thursday, a month before his 89th birthday. Hockney was acclaimed worldwide.

items.title: Media practitioners call on govt to provide healthcare, welfare aid for industry veterans
items.summary: IPOH, June 12 — The government is urged to consider introducing a dedicated aid mechanism for veteran or former media practitioners in recognition of their contributions to the nation’s journalism industry, while also helping to ease their post-retirement financial burdens. He said the move would not only help reduce medical costs but also serve as a gesture of appreciation for media practitioners who have greatly contributed to information dissemination and national development. “Some retired media practitioners face various health issues due to age and work-related stress throughout their careers.

items.title: Perlis Education Dept pledges full cooperation with police after pupil injured in chair-pulling incident
items.summary: KANGAR, June 12 — The Perlis State Education Department (JPN) is giving its full cooperation to the police investigation into a case involving a Year Six pupil who was injured after allegedly falling when a chair she was about to sit on was pulled away by a classmate at a school in the state last February. In a statement today, JPN Perlis said it would not compromise on bullying misconduct and that anyone found guilty should face appropriate action. “JPN Perlis takes seriously this incident at a school that resulted in injuries to a pupil,” it said, adding that the school concerned had been instructed to ensure the best possible assistance is provided to the pupil, whose welfare and well-being must be given priority.

items.title: Court clears way for auction of Jamal Yunos’ seized assets after failure to pay Theresa Kok
items.summary: Lawyer Datuk SN Nair, who represented Kok, said the High Court today dismissed the interpleader claim application submitted by Jamal’s son, Muhammad Amin Daniel, who claimed that the seized items belonged to him. “Muhammad Amin Daniel’s application was dismissed with costs of RM8,000. Jamal paid the damages.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia’s strength lies in managing differences fairly, religion must promote peace
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Malaysia’s strength lies not in uniformity but in its ability to manage differences fairly and justly, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. Anwar also urged religious leaders to continue using their moral authority and influence to promote peace and justice, saying their role has become increasingly important amid growing global conflicts and humanitarian crises. Anwar said such developments only underscored the growing importance of religious leaders in advocating reason, tolerance and moral responsibility in an increasingly uncertain world.

items.title: Fahmi disappointed as Lee Chean Chung quits PKR council, says voters deserve answers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today expressed disappointment over the resignation of Petaling Jaya MP Lee Chean Chung from the party’s central leadership council, saying that he owed an explanation to his voters and party members. Fahmi said he had known Lee for many years and believed the MP should have used the party’s internal platforms to raise any concerns rather than leaving the party. He said differing views within PKR were not uncommon and pointed to senior party leader Elizabeth Wong, who is also the former member of Selangor State Executive Council as an example of someone who regularly expressed views that differed from the party leadership while continuing to engage through party channels.

items.title: Hospitalisation, critical illness and medical device aid among RM1.42b MySalam payouts to Malaysians, says minister
items.summary: According to figures presented to the Cabinet, he said over 1.294 million recipients have received hospitalisation benefits amounting to RM394.87 million. “For critical illness claims, 89,085 recipients received payments amounting to RM712.68 million. For medical device assistance, 13,128 recipients received support worth RM65.257 million,” he added.

items.title: Amirudin says Selangor will act against all illegal settlements without considering nationality
items.summary: KUALA SELANGOR, June 12 — The Selangor government will take action against all illegal settlements in the state, including demolition, regardless of the occupants’ nationality, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said. He said enforcement measures are not based on sentiment or nationality but on land administration requirements, land-use conditions and compliance with local authority regulations. However, he said the state government must adhere to legal procedures, including giving landowners sufficient time to rectify breaches before stricter measures such as land forfeiture are imposed.

items.title: Fahmi: Putrajaya to collect detailed workforce data before deciding on foreign worker quotas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the government will gather more detailed workforce data before making any decisions on foreign worker quotas in the restaurant and construction sectors. He said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had directed several ministries to engage with industry players and obtain a clearer picture of labour requirements. “As Malaysia currently maintains a policy on limiting the overall number of foreign workers, the prime minister directed the Human Resources Ministry, the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry, the Housing and Local Government Ministry, and the Works Ministry to obtain more detailed information from industry players regarding labour requirements, including demand for foreign workers,” he said.

items.title: PKR Youth says opposition blaming Anwar over internal turmoil is political distraction
items.summary: IPOH, June 12 — Angkatan Muda Keadilan (AMK) has described attempts to link Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to turmoil within the Opposition as a desperate effort to conceal the bloc’s leadership weaknesses. AMK chief Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim said it was unreasonable to blame Anwar for every internal crisis affecting the Opposition, and that the issues actually stemmed from power struggles and disputes over seat allocations. “Marzuki (Datuk Marzuki Mohamad) himself admitted that internal turmoil exists within the Opposition due to seat disputes and differing political interests.

items.title: Malaysians spend 15.3pc more in April to RM174.8b, but goods bought rise just 6.2pc, DOSM reports
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 12 — Malaysians spent more in April as wholesale and retail trade sales rose 15.3 per cent year-on-year to RM174.8 billion, outpacing a 6.2 per cent increase in the volume of goods sold, according to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM). Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Mohd Uzir Mahidin said wholesale trade recorded sales of RM83.5 billion in April, an increase of 24.1 per cent and a volume growth of 7.3 per cent, compared with the same month last year. He said retail trade registered sales of RM71.0 billion, an increase of 6.3 per cent or RM4.2 billion year-on-year.

items.title: Perak police chief: Complaints against Rohingya should be channelled through authorities
items.summary: IPOH, June 12 — The public is advised not to take action against the Rohingya ethnic group themselves but rather channel any complaints or relevant information to the police for appropriate action. Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said any misconduct by the Rohingya ethnic group should be reported to the authorities and action will be taken in accordance with the existing law. “We will always conduct regular monitoring of the Rohingya ethnic group,” he said.

items.title: Selangor freezes ‘trans hiking’ and ‘hiking compress’ permits after string of recent incidents
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — The Selangor State Forestry Department has suspended all applications for “trans hiking” and “hiking compress” activities across the state’s Permanent Forest Reserves, effective July 1. The suspension targets high-risk, trail-based activities, particularly multi-day or cross-country treks. The suspension specifically applies to “trans hiking” and “hiking compress” itineraries, activities that typically involve traversing remote sections of forest reserves, overnight stays, and limited emergency accessibility.

items.title: Unilateral child conversion laws in six states stand as High Court dismisses challenge by Indira Gandhi, 13 others
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — The High Court today dismissed a challenge by Ipoh mother M. Indira Gandhi and 13 others against state laws that allow children under age 18 to be converted to Islam without both parents’ consent. Court of Appeal judge Datuk Aliza Sulaiman, who was a High Court judge when this case was heard, said that all 14 of them had failed to show they have locus standi, meaning the legal standing, to pursue this court challenge. But as this court challenge was filed as a public interest matter, the judge did not order Indira and the 13 others to pay for legal costs.

items.title: Two small parties, one big decision: Hadi puts Gerakan and MIPP at centre of Perikatan power play
items.summary: MARANG, June 12 — PAS will decide on Bersatu’s future in Perikatan Nasional (PN) at its central working committee meeting next Wednesday, party president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang confirmed today, days after political cooperation between the coalition’s two biggest parties collapsed. Hadi said any expulsion from PN must be backed by a majority of the coalition’s four component parties: PAS, Bersatu, Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People’s Party (MIPP). Hadi said PAS would leave it to both parties to choose between PAS and Bersatu in charting the coalition’s direction.

items.title: Police arrest 12, including 10 Chinese nationals, in Melaka scam call centre bust
items.summary: MELAKA, June 12 — The police dismantled a ‘call centre’ syndicate suspected of being involved in a non-existent online investment scam with the arrest of 12 individuals in two raids at Taman Krubong Perdana here early on Wednesday morning. Acting Melaka Tengah district police chief Supt Halim Abas said that those detained comprised 10 male Chinese nationals, in addition to a local man and woman, all aged between 23 and 47 years. “In the first raid at 2.30 am, a team from the Melaka Commercial Crime Investigation Department (JSJK) arrested six individuals, consisting of five male Chinese nationals and one local man, all aged between 23 and 43 years.

items.title: Kedah schoolgirl unable to sit or stand since classmate’s chair-pulling prank in February
items.summary: PADANG BESAR, June 12 — A 12-year-old girl has allegedly been unable to attend school for nearly four months after sustaining injuries in a fall when the chair she was about to sit on was allegedly pulled away by a classmate during a lesson in February. Her mother, Norasikin Hasin, 32, claimed that the Year Six pupil suffered a soft tissue injury to her lower back and was subsequently diagnosed with functional neurological disorder and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) following the incident on Feb 11. She said the incident occurred during a teaching and learning session when her daughter attempted to sit down after greeting a teacher, but fell to the floor after her chair was allegedly pulled away by a female classmate.
